content.problemStatement:
Chef has two integers AAA and BBB. In one operation he can choose any integer d,d,d, and make one of the following two moves :


Add ddd to AAA and subtract ddd from BBB.
Add ddd to BBB and subtract ddd from AAA.
Chef is allowed to make as many operations as he wants. Can he make AAA and BBB **equal**?


Input Format

First line will contain TTT, number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
Each test case contains of a single line of input, two integers A,BA, BA,B.


Output Format
For each test case, if Chef can make the two numbers equal print `YES` else print `NO`.


You may print each character of the string in uppercase or lowercase (for example, the strings `yEs`, `Yes`, `YeS`, and `YES` will all be treated as identical).

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: Since AAA and BBB are already equal, Chef does not need any operations.
Test case 222: It can be shown that AAA and BBB can never be made equal using any number of given operations.
